node.title: Uber Technical Phone Screen | Machine Learning Engineer role

topic.title: Uber Technical Phone Screen | Machine Learning Engineer role

post.content:
Hi all,
I am in the interview process for Machine Learning Engineer role at Uber - Toronto Canada. 

First round was CodeSignal assessment which I passed with a score of 839.

Next step is Technical Phone Screen of 1 hour. Recruiter has told me that it'd comprise of a coding round with 1 to 2 questions for around 35-40 mins and then theoritical/fundamental ML questions for the rest of the time.

Can anyone who has gone through this process guide me on what the level of the questions they typically ask for coding questions and what are the typical ML questions they ask? Is it more background related or the team related?

Thanks in advance!

#uber #onsite #technicalscreen #machinelearning #coding

For me, coding was a LC Medium problem with a few follow-up questions around how my solution would scale, etc. That lasted for like 45min. Then a very brief 5-10min of "walk me through one of your recent ML projects" with some basic questions like how did you tune parameters, etc (very easy). So my advise would be spend more time preparing for the coding portion if you know your fundamental ML. Although things could always vary interview to interview :)
How was the onsite for you guys?
how was the codesignal round?leetocde easy or med or hard?
